2026-04-22T16:40:53.7225570Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7226104Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7226516Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7226870Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7227164Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7227412Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7227571Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7227755Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7227922Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7228142Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7228340Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7228617Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T16:40:53.7228870Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. These samples are now invisible to users browsing the in-package samples index.\\n- `samples/servicebus/dead-letter-queue/README.md` still says **\\u0026#34;Install [.NET 5.0]\\u0026#34;** — this is outdated; should reference .NET 10 (consistent with the IoT Hub sample that was updated in the same PR).\\n- `samples/servicebus/topic-filters/README.md` still says **\\u0026#34;Install [.NET 5.0]\\u0026#34;** — same issue.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/servicebus/Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus/samples/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — append the following three entries to the end of the sample list (after the existing `Sample20_PerformancePatterns.md` link):\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Application samples\\n\\nThese samples demonstrate end-to-end application scenarios using Azure Service Bus.\\n\\n- [Dead-letter queues](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/samples/servicebus/dead-letter-queue/README.md)\\n- [Topic filters](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/samples/servicebus/topic-filters/README.md)\\n- [Plugin extensibility pattern](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/samples/servicebus/plugin-sample/README.md)\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `samples/servicebus/dead-letter-queue/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — replace the stale .NET version reference. Install [.NET 10.0](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) or newer.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 3: `samples/servicebus/topic-filters/README.md`\\n\\n**Edit** — replace the stale .NET version reference. Install [.NET 10.0](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) or newer.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are required for these changes. The samples are standalone projects and are already complete; only the index README links and the version strings need updating.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe existing `sdk/servicebus/Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus/samples/README.md` is already a flat link list — no required section structure applies. The three new standalone sample READMEs (`dead-letter-queue`, `topic-filters`, `plugin-sample`) each have the correct front-matter and sections. No restructuring is needed; only the link additions and version string fixes described above.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/servicebus/Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/servicebus/Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `dotnet build samples/servicebus/dead-letter-queue/`\\n4. `dotnet build samples/servicebus/topic-filters/`\\n5. `dotnet build samples/servicebus/plugin-sample/`\\n\\nNo snippet regeneration or API export is required as no public types or members were added.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-22T16:44:44.1911422Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T16:42:45.044466161Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":219,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":3732908692,\"number\":54541,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[FEATURE REQ] Unified execution contract for retry, timeout, and cancellation semantics across Azure .NET SDKs\",\"body\":\"In large-scale, **distributed .NET applications** that **use multiple Azure .NET SDKs** (for example Service Bus, Event Hubs, Storage, Cosmos DB, Key Vault,), retry behavior, timeout enforcement, and **CancellationToken semantics** are currently inconsistent and difficult to reason about across SDK boundaries.\\n\\nEach SDK exposes retry options and accepts a CancellationToken, but the combined behavior across async/await boundaries, background processing, transport retries, and SDK-internal retry policies is non-deterministic in practice. This makes it difficult to enforce strict latency budgets, bounded retries, and predictable failure behavior in real-world distributed systems.\\n\\nIn particular:\\n\\n- Retry policies may execute at multiple layers without a clear, unified contract for total retries or total elapsed time.\\n- Timeout settings appear to be interpreted differently across SDKs, with no clear guidance on per-attempt versus global timeout behavior.\\n- CancellationToken propagation is inconsistent, sometimes stopping user code but not in-flight retries or transport operations.\\n\\nIt would be valuable to introduce or document a **unified execution contract across Azure .NET SDKs** that clearly defines how retries, timeouts, and cancellation interact. This could include guidance or primitives for enforcing a global execution budget, deterministic cancellation behavior, and consistent retry semantics across SDKs.\\n\\n**1. Sample code snippet: global execution budget wrapper (SDK-agnostic)**\\n\\n```csharp\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Diagnostics;\\nusing System.Threading;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\n\\npublic static class ExecutionBudget\\n{\\n    // A global time budget that you expect ALL nested SDK calls to respect.\\n    public static async Task\\u003cT\\u003e RunAsync\\u003cT\\u003e(\\n        Func\\u003cCancellationToken, Task\\u003cT\\u003e\\u003e operation,\\n        TimeSpan budget,\\n        CancellationToken callerToken = default)\\n    {\\n        using var budgetCts = new CancellationTokenSource(budget);\\n        using var linkedCts = CancellationTokenSource.CreateLinkedTokenSource(callerToken, budgetCts.Token);\\n\\n        var sw = Stopwatch.StartNew();\\n        try\\n        {\\n            return await operation(linkedCts.Token).ConfigureAwait(false);\\n        }\\n        finally\\n        {\\n            sw.Stop();\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\\"ExecutionBudget elapsed: {sw.Elapsed.TotalMilliseconds:n0} ms (budget: {budget.TotalMilliseconds:n0} ms)\\\");\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**2. Sample code snippet: two SDK calls under one budget (illustrative)**\\n\\n```csharp\\nusing Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus;\\nusing Azure.Storage.Blobs;\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\n\\npublic class MultiSdkBudgetExample\\n{\\n    public async Task RunAsync(\\n        BlobClient blob,\\n        ServiceBusSender sender,\\n        CancellationToken token)\\n    {\\n        await ExecutionBudget.RunAsync(async ct =\\u003e\\n        {\\n            // Call 1: Storage (HTTP)\\n            await blob.GetPropertiesAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]            // Call 2: Service Bus (AMQP)\\n            var msg = new ServiceBusMessage(\\\"test\\\") { MessageId = Guid.NewGuid().ToString(\\\"N\\\") };\\n            await sender.SendMessageAsync(msg, ct);\\n\\n            return 0;\\n        }, budget: TimeSpan.FromSeconds(3), callerToken=[REDACTED]    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**3. Sample config snippet: showing “retry knobs differ” across SDKs**\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar sbOptions = new ServiceBusClientOptions\\n{\\n    RetryOptions =\\n    {\\n        Mode = ServiceBusRetryMode.Exponential,\\n        MaxRetries = 5,\\n        Delay = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(200),\\n        MaxDelay = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5),\\n        TryTimeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(10) // important semantic question: per-try or global?\\n    }\\n};\\n```\\n\\nThis is not a request for changes to a specific SDK, but a cross-cutting design consideration intended to improve predictability, reliability, and operability in distributed, event-driven, and latency-sensitive systems.\",\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"MahendhiranK\",\"id\":58991247,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjU4OTkxMjQ3\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/58991247?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/MahendhiranK\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/MahendhiranK\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/MahendhiranK/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/MahendhiranK/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/MahendhiranK/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/MahendhiranK/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/MahendhiranK/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/MahendhiranK/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/MahendhiranK/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/MahendhiranK/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/MahendhiranK/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1178447860,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Client\",\"name\":\"Client\",\"color\":\"ffeb77\",\"description\":\"This issue is related to a non-management package\",\"default\":false,\"node_id\":\"MDU6TGFiZWwxMTc4NDQ3ODYw\"},{\"id\":1225887848,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Azure.Core\",\"name\":\"Azure.Core\",\"color\":\"e99695\",\"description\":\"\",\"default\":false,\"node_id\":\"MDU6TGFiZWwxMjI1ODg3ODQ4\"},{\"id\":1353316183,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/customer-reported\",\"name\":\"customer-reported\",\"color\":\"3800e0\",\"description\":\"Issues issue doesn't require a change to the product in order to be resolved.
